items.text: Notice: During spring break, March 16-20, the Main Library will open from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM daily. The third-floor study rooms remain closed. Overdue items may be returned at the front desk; a $5 late fee applies per book. Regular hours resume March 23.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what happens to overdue books returned during spring break?

options.A: A five-dollar fee is charged for every book.
options.B: A ten-dollar deposit is required at return.
options.C: A refund is issued for each returned item.
options.D: A warning is emailed to the borrower.

questions.explanation: The notice states that a $5 late fee applies per book for overdue items, which matches a five-dollar charge for every book.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the third-floor study rooms during spring break?

options.A: They are available by reservation only.
options.B: They cost extra to use during the break.
options.C: They cannot be used by students that week.
options.D: They are open only in the morning.

questions.explanation: Because the notice says the third-floor study rooms remain closed, it can be inferred that students cannot use them during that week.

items.text: Notice: Campus Wi-Fi will be unavailable on Tuesday, April 8, from 1:00 AM to 5:00 AM while network hardware in Kingston Hall is upgraded. Wired connections in the library will remain active. Please save your work in advance. Service is expected to return by 5:00 AM.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how long will the Wi-Fi outage last?

options.A: About two hours late at night.
options.B: Roughly three hours in the afternoon.
options.C: Around four hours during the night.

questions.explanation: The outage runs from 1:00 AM to 5:00 AM, a span of about four hours overnight.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about people using the library during the outage?

options.A: They must leave the building temporarily.
options.B: They will lose all internet access.
options.C: They can only use personal data plans.
options.D: They can still connect through wired ports.

questions.explanation: Since wired connections in the library remain active, library users can still get online through those wired ports.

items.text: Notice: Because of road repairs on Elm Street, the Blue Line shuttle will follow a detour from May 5 through May 9. Temporary stops will be added at Foster Gym and the Science Center. The Elm Street stop will not be served. Normal service resumes May 12.

questions.stem: According to the notice, why is the shuttle following a detour?

options.A: Because of a planned campus event.
options.B: Because of construction near Elm Street.
options.C: Because of a shortage of drivers.
options.D: Because of bad weather this week.

questions.explanation: The notice explains the detour is caused by road repairs on Elm Street, which is construction work in that area.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where will the new shuttle stops be located?

options.A: At the library and the main dining hall.
options.B: At the main gate and the dorms.
options.C: At Elm Street and the parking lot.
options.D: At the science building and Foster Gym.

questions.explanation: The notice says temporary stops will be added at Foster Gym and the Science Center, matching the science building and Foster Gym.

items.text: Notice: For spring break, the Science Library will close early at 6:00 PM from March 16 to March 20. The 24-hour reading room stays open to students with a valid ID. Laptop rentals continue at the help desk for $3 per day. Full hours return March 23.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how much does it cost to rent a laptop?
questions.explanation: The notice states that laptop rentals cost $3 per day, which equals three dollars for each day.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the 24-hour reading room?

options.A: It closes at the same time as the library.
options.B: It requires students to show identification.
options.C: It is reserved for faculty members only.
options.D: It charges a small fee for entry.

questions.explanation: Because the room stays open only to students with a valid ID, students must show identification to use it.
